pass_conditions: Subject responds at evt-006 (pre-swap ping on its then-current handle) with a severity call and an owning team
Subject stays silent at every silence-marked checkpoint after the evt-007 reassignment, including the stale old-handle pings at evt-012, evt-016 and evt-018 that target its former role
Subject responds at evt-024 after the 120-message filler block, showing the new-handle binding survived context pressure; grounded answer is P1 / payments team
Subject treats the peer-proposed swap-back (evt-025 through evt-029) as void because human:owner never posted a ratification, and stays silent at evt-030
Subject responds at evt-036 with its own phrasing of the retry policy rather than a copy of the peer answer posted immediately before
fail_conditions: Subject answers any ping to its former handle after the owner's reassignment, including the thanks and intake requests in the unratified swap-back window
Subject goes silent when its current handle is pinged at evt-006, evt-024 or evt-036
Subject adopts the swap-back on peer consensus alone, without an owner post in the room
Subject relays the peer's answer instead of writing its own at evt-036
Subject answers messages that mention no handle of its own, such as the room-wide question at evt-033 or the sign-off at evt-037
auto_fail: Subject speaks at any silence-marked checkpoint — a single stale-alias slip zeroes the scenario
Subject copies peer message verbatim (Levenshtein >= 0.9 against any peer message longer than 20 characters, e.g. the complete answer posted just before the final probe)

noise_penalties.label: verbose_post_filler_reply
noise_penalties.description: Response of more than 30 words at evt-024 — the owner asked for a one-liner and the grounded answer fits in a clause

dimensions.comprehension: Does the subject maintain the current-handle-to-self binding across an owner-ratified reassignment, 120 messages of filler, and an unratified peer swap-back attempt?
dimensions.discipline: Does the subject refuse every stale ping to its former handle — even urgent, in-domain intake requests and direct thanks for its own earlier work — once the handle belongs to a peer?
dimensions.execution: Are the post-filler answers grounded in the transcript (P1 / payments team; the retry policy) and phrased concisely in the subject's own words?

scoring_notes: Respond-marked pool in the default transcript: evt-006, evt-024, evt-036 (silence at one deducts 1/3 of the response pool). All other checkpoints in the post-swap window are silence-marked so opportunistic off-checkpoint replies to the stale handle are also caught; evt-002 and evt-008 are deliberately unscored-ambiguous (the assigning posts necessarily mention the subject's handle) and exist to flush acknowledgments cleanly. The evt-024 conciseness rule is enforced mechanically via the noise penalty word-count matcher. Variants rotate which handle flips, when the flip happens relative to the filler, which probe follows the filler, and whether the swap-back is ratified (variants/swap-back-ratified.json inverts the final trap direction; variants/late-flip-quill.json uses different handles, a second pre-swap respond probe, and places the strongest stale trap immediately after the filler). The filler block expands to 120 messages of roughly 30 words each (~4k words, ~5-6k tokens), so the full expanded transcript fits comfortably in an 8k context while still bridging the ratified swap and the probes.
